name: Asynq Go Task Queue Library
what_it_does: Simple, reliable, and efficient distributed task queue library for Go backed by Redis — providing background job processing, scheduled tasks, and task retry with built-in web UI for monitoring queues and workers.
use_cases: Processing background tasks in Go applications with Redis as the queue backend
Scheduling recurring tasks and delayed job execution in Go microservices
Building distributed task processing systems across multiple worker instances
Email sending, image processing, and data pipeline tasks in Go applications
Agent task queuing for Go-based AI agent orchestration systems
not_for: Applications without Redis infrastructure or preference for PostgreSQL-native queues
Very high-frequency scheduling at millisecond precision (use Quartz Scheduler for that)
best_when: A Go application needs reliable distributed task processing with Redis, monitoring UI, and clean API without the complexity of larger message brokers.
avoid_when: You use Python, Node.js, or Ruby, or prefer PostgreSQL-native queuing without Redis.

auth.notes: Redis connection auth (password or ACL). Asynqmon web UI has optional HTTP Basic auth. No separate API auth — inherits Redis security. Redis TLS for encrypted connections.

pricing.free_tier_limits: MIT open source — completely free; no limits
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tier — pure open source
pricing.notes: MIT open source — completely free. Popular Go background job library with active development. Includes Asynqmon web UI for queue monitoring. Production-ready with battle-tested Redis backend.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — limited by Redis throughput (millions of ops/sec)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Go SDK with comprehensive documentation and examples. Asynqmon HTTP API for queue inspection. Type-safe task definition with Go structs. Good error handling with retry policies.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Task deduplication via unique task ID. At-least-once delivery by default — implement idempotent handlers. Task processing guaranteed exactly-once with proper Redis Lua scripting.
known_agent_gotchas: Go only — not suitable for Python or other language agents
Redis required — adds Redis infrastructure dependency
At-least-once delivery means handlers must be idempotent
Task payload serialized to JSON — complex types need careful marshaling

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open source — fully auditable. Security inherits from Redis — configure TLS and auth. Task data stored in Redis — consider encryption for sensitive payloads. Asynqmon UI should be restricted in production.
